title: Classic Gingerbread Cookies
description: The definitive gingerbread recipe — crisp edges, chewy centers, and a spice blend that fills the entire house.
imageAlt: Freshly baked gingerbread cookies on a cooling rack
instructions: Whisk flour, baking powder, baking soda, ginger, cinnamon, cloves, nutmeg, and salt in a large bowl.
In a separate bowl, cream butter and brown sugar until light and fluffy, about 3 minutes.
Beat in the egg, then add molasses and vanilla. Mix until smooth.
Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture, mixing on low until just combined.
Divide dough in half, flatten into discs, wrap in plastic, and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
Roll dough to 1/4-inch thickness on a floured surface. Cut into shapes.
Bake 10-12 minutes until edges are firm but centers still look slightly soft.
Cool on baking sheet for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack.
tips: Chill the dough overnight for the best flavor — the spices deepen as they rest.
For softer cookies, pull them at 10 minutes. For snappier ones, go the full 12.
Use a mix of dark and light molasses for complexity.
These freeze beautifully — bake a double batch and freeze half for cookie emergencies.

title: Fireside Hot Chocolate
description: Rich, dark, and impossibly velvety — this is the hot chocolate that ruins all other hot chocolates for you.
imageAlt: Steaming mug of dark hot chocolate with marshmallows
instructions: Heat milk and cream in a heavy saucepan over medium heat until steaming but not boiling.
Remove from heat. Add chopped chocolate and let sit for 1 minute undisturbed.
Whisk gently until chocolate is completely melted and smooth.
Sift in cocoa powder and sugar. Whisk until no lumps remain.
Return to low heat and warm through for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
Remove from heat, add vanilla, salt, and cinnamon. Whisk once more.
Pour into warmed mugs. Top with whipped cream, marshmallows, or a cinnamon stick.
tips: The better the chocolate, the better the cocoa. Use something you'd eat on its own.
Warm your mugs with hot water first — the cocoa stays hot much longer.
For a Mexican twist, add a pinch of cayenne and extra cinnamon.
Leftover cocoa reheats beautifully the next day on the stovetop.

title: Spiced Mulled Cider
description: The smell alone is worth making this. Slow-simmered apple cider with warming spices and a hint of orange.
imageAlt: Glass mugs of warm mulled cider with cinnamon sticks and orange slices
instructions: Pour apple cider into a large pot or slow cooker.
Add orange slices, cinnamon sticks, cloves, star anise, allspice, and ginger.
Bring to a gentle simmer over medium-low heat. Do not boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 25-30 minutes.
Stir in maple syrup and vanilla extract.
Strain through a fine mesh sieve into a warmed pitcher or serve directly with a ladle.
Garnish each mug with a cinnamon stick and orange slice.
tips: Use unfiltered cider for the best flavor — the cloudier, the better.
This keeps warm in a slow cooker on low for hours, making it perfect for parties.
For an adult version, add 2 oz bourbon or dark rum per mug.
The spices can be reused once — save the cinnamon sticks for a second batch.
